_comment: Auto-updated by discovery-feedback-learner.py. Stores triage outcomes and Gemini-derived scoring adjustments. Do not edit the 'learned' section manually — it is overwritten on each learner run. The 'overrides' section IS safe to edit manually to pin specific corrections.

learned._comment: Gemini-derived scoring adjustments. Applied on top of google-search-config.json heuristics at discovery time. Weights are additive deltas relative to the base config values.

signal_weight_deltas._comment: Keys match positive_signals / negative_signals in google-search-config.json. Values are additive adjustments to apply at score time.

domain_blocklist._comment: Domains repeatedly producing false positives. Results from these domains are hard-rejected during scoring, same as url_reject_patterns.

domain_trustlist._comment: Domains that consistently produce true positives. These receive an additional bonus on top of org_domain_match_bonus.

org_specific_signals._comment: Per-org positive/negative keyword overrides. Key is lowercase org name. Merged with global signals at score time.

title_patterns._comment: Regex patterns Gemini identified as reliable false-positive indicators. Any result whose title matches one of these is hard-rejected.

learned.score_threshold_delta_comment: Added to the base score_threshold in google-search-config.json. Positive = stricter. Negative = looser. Gemini adjusts this when the FP rate is high.

overrides._comment: Human-pinned corrections that survive learner rewrites. These take highest precedence over both base config and learned values.

org_aliases._comment: Maps alternate org names found in issue titles back to a canonical org name for dedup matching. E.g. 'MSFT' -> 'Microsoft'.

hard_reject_urls._comment: Specific URLs that must always be rejected regardless of score. Useful for known-bad mirrors or aggregators.
